chapters.title: — Cold Open: The Annotation Problem, Named
— Headline: Citation, Design, and the Choice That Earns the Evidence Grade
— Headline: Numbers, Heterogeneity, and What "Six Cohorts" Does and Doesn't Prove
— Headline: Practice Impact — Honest About "Nothing Yet"
— Pivot and Round 1: Landry, Meningioma H&E Classification
— Round 2: de Bie, Focal Laser Ablation Registry
— Round 4: Zhang, Paraspinal Muscle Fracture Prediction
— Close: What to Watch, What's Unsettled, Sign-Off

follow_up_links.reason: Demonstrates that weakly supervised contrastive learning can maintain consistent prostate cancer detection across six heterogeneous MRI cohorts without pixel-level annotation
follow_up_links.title: Enhancing foundation model transfer for prostate cancer detection with patch-level contrastive learning

follow_up_links.reason: Shows that routine histology slides can predict meningioma molecular subtypes and recurrence, potentially reducing reliance on genomic testing
follow_up_links.title: Deep learning for H&E-based meningioma molecular classification and outcome prediction

follow_up_links.reason: Provides multicenter registry data on 12-month oncological outcomes for image-guided focal therapy, informing patient selection discussions
follow_up_links.title: One-year follow-up after US-guided transperineal focal laser ablation of localized prostate cancer

follow_up_links.reason: SKELEX demonstrates that foundation models pretrained on over a million radiographs can outperform task-specific models across diverse MSK diagnostic tasks
follow_up_links.title: A large-scale vision foundation model for musculoskeletal radiographs

follow_up_links.reason: Automated paraspinal muscle analysis on MRI predicts vertebral fracture risk better than clinical models alone, suggesting opportunistic screening potential
follow_up_links.title: Fully automated system predicts osteoporotic vertebral fracture using lumbar MRI paraspinal muscle signatures

key_claims.claim: Weakly supervised contrastive learning using patch-level representations enables consistent prostate cancer detection on MRI across six independent cohorts without requiring lesion-level annotation
Deep learning applied to standard H&E histology slides can predict meningioma molecular subtypes and recurrence risk with performance validated across three institutions
Transperineal focal laser ablation for localized prostate cancer shows acceptable 12-month oncological outcomes in a multicenter registry
A foundation model pretrained on 1.2 million musculoskeletal radiographs outperforms task-specific baselines across 12 diagnostic tasks including fracture and tumor detection
Automated MRI paraspinal muscle segmentation predicts osteoporotic vertebral fracture with AUC 0.84 across five institutions, exceeding clinical-only models
